title: New Genetic Test Could Help Millions Avoid Chemotherapy
subtitle: A major international study shows that many breast cancer patients can safely skip harsh treatments by using advanced gene analysis.

summary.en: A new international study has found that a genomic test can identify breast cancer patients who do not need chemotherapy. The trial followed over 4,000 patients across several countries to see if they could safely rely on hormone therapy alone. Results showed that patients with low-risk test scores had excellent survival rates without the need for toxic drugs. This breakthrough could change how doctors treat the most common form of breast cancer worldwide. Experts believe this represents a significant shift toward more personalized and efficient medical care.
summary.zh: 一項新的國際研究發現，基因體檢測可以辨識出不需要化學治療的乳癌患者。這項試驗追蹤了橫跨多國、超過四千名患者，以觀察他們是否能安全地僅依賴荷爾蒙治療。結果顯示，檢測分數為低風險的患者，在無需使用毒性藥物的情況下，仍擁有極佳的存活率。這項突破可能改變全球醫師治療最常見乳癌類型的方式。專家認為，這代表醫療照護正顯著轉向更個人化與更有效率的方向。

paragraphs.en: For millions of women diagnosed with breast cancer, the path to recovery often includes chemotherapy. While this treatment is effective at preventing the disease from returning, it also comes with difficult side effects. Patients often suffer from hair loss, extreme tiredness, and physical pain. Some may even face long-term health issues, such as early menopause or problems with memory and thinking. For decades, doctors have had few ways to know exactly which patients truly need this intense treatment and which could safely avoid it.
paragraphs.zh: 對於數百萬名確診乳癌的女性而言，康復之路往往包含化學治療。雖然這種治療在預防疾病復發方面相當有效，但也伴隨著艱難的副作用。患者經常飽受掉髮、極度疲勞與身體疼痛之苦。有些人甚至面臨長期的健康問題，例如提早進入更年期，或是記憶力與思考能力受損。數十年來，醫師幾乎沒有方法能精確判斷哪些患者真正需要這種強效治療，而哪些人可以安全地免除化療。

paragraphs.en: However, a new international study, known as the Optima trial, suggests that a groundbreaking genomic test could change everything. The test, called Prosigna, looks at the activity of 50 specific genes within a patient’s tumour. By analyzing these genes, the test determines the “molecular subtype” of the cancer—essentially its biological makeup—and provides a score that tells doctors how likely the cancer is to return within the next ten years. According to The Guardian Health, this information helps medical teams decide if chemotherapy is necessary or if a patient can be treated with hormone therapy alone.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，一項名為「Optima」的國際研究顯示，一項突破性的基因體檢測或許能改變這一切。這項名為「Prosigna」的檢測會觀察患者腫瘤內五十種特定基因的活性。透過分析這些基因，檢測能判定癌症的「分子亞型」（本質上就是其生物組成），並提供一項分數，告知醫師該癌症在未來十年內復發的可能性。根據《衛報健康版》（The Guardian Health）報導，這項資訊能協助醫療團隊決定化療是否必要，或是患者是否僅需接受荷爾蒙治療即可。

paragraphs.en: The trial was large and diverse, involving 4,429 patients aged 40 or older from countries including the UK, Norway, Sweden, Australia, New Zealand, and Thailand. All participants had a common form of breast cancer that responds to hormones, which makes up about 80% of all breast cancer cases globally. The researchers split the participants into two groups. One group received the standard treatment of chemotherapy followed by hormone therapy. The other group had their tumours analyzed by the genomic test. If the test showed a low risk, those patients skipped chemotherapy and used only hormone therapy.
paragraphs.zh: 這項試驗規模龐大且具多樣性，涵蓋了來自英國、挪威、瑞典、澳洲、紐西蘭與泰國等地，共四千四百二十九名四十歲以上的患者。所有參與者皆罹患一種常見且對荷爾蒙有反應的乳癌，這類病例約佔全球乳癌總數的八成。研究人員將參與者分為兩組：一組接受標準的化療後接續荷爾蒙治療；另一組則透過基因體檢測分析腫瘤。若檢測顯示風險較低，這些患者便可跳過化療，僅使用荷爾蒙治療。

paragraphs.en: The results, which are being presented at the American Society of Clinical Oncology’s annual meeting in Chicago, are very positive. The Guardian Health reports that the five-year survival rate for the group that skipped chemotherapy was 93.7%. This was statistically very similar to the 94.9% survival rate seen in the group that received chemotherapy. These findings suggest that many women can safely avoid the physical and emotional burden of chemotherapy without increasing the risk of their cancer coming back.
paragraphs.zh: 這些在芝加哥舉行的美國臨床腫瘤學會（American Society of Clinical Oncology）年會上發表的研究結果非常正面。《衛報健康版》報導指出，跳過化療的那一組，其五年存活率為百分之九十三點七。這在統計學上與接受化療組的百分之九十四點九存活率極為相近。這些發現顯示，許多女性可以在不增加癌症復發風險的情況下，安全地免除化療帶來的生理與心理負擔。

paragraphs.en: For many patients, this news brings a sense of great relief. Karen Bonham, a 64-year-old retired speech and language therapist from Wales, was one of the participants in the trial. She was diagnosed with breast cancer in 2017 and was only days away from starting chemotherapy when she received a phone call from her hospital. The results of her genomic test showed that she did not need the treatment. Describing her reaction to The Guardian Health, she said the news felt “like Christmas.” Today, nine years after her diagnosis, she remains healthy, active, and free of cancer. She credits the trial for allowing her to live a full life without the long-term effects of chemotherapy.
paragraphs.zh: 對許多患者而言，這項消息帶來了極大的寬慰。來自威爾斯的六十四歲退休語言治療師凱倫·邦漢（Karen Bonham）是該試驗的參與者之一。她在二零一七年確診乳癌，就在準備開始化療的前幾天，她接到了醫院的電話。基因體檢測結果顯示她並不需要接受該治療。她在向《衛報健康版》描述當時的反應時表示，那個消息感覺「就像聖誕節一樣」。如今，在確診九年後，她依然健康、活躍且沒有癌症。她將自己能過上充實生活且免受化療長期影響的功勞，歸功於這項試驗。

paragraphs.en: Prof Rob Stein, the chief investigator of the trial and a professor of breast oncology at University College London, explained that this study addresses a long-standing challenge in cancer care. He noted that the findings allow doctors to use tumour biology to guide their decisions, rather than relying only on traditional clinical methods. By using this evidence-based approach, health systems can use their resources more effectively, and patients can avoid unnecessary, toxic treatments.
paragraphs.zh: 該試驗的首席研究員、倫敦大學學院（University College London）乳癌腫瘤學教授羅伯·史坦（Rob Stein）解釋，這項研究解決了癌症照護中長期存在的一項挑戰。他指出，這些發現讓醫師能運用腫瘤生物學來引導決策，而非僅依賴傳統的臨床方法。透過這種實證導向的方法，醫療體系能更有效地運用資源，患者也能避免不必要的毒性治療。

paragraphs.en: It is important to note that this test is not for everyone. The Guardian Health clarifies that for some patients, chemotherapy remains a necessary and important part of their treatment plan. The test is a tool to help doctors make better, more personalized decisions based on the specific biology of a patient's cancer. As the medical community looks toward the future, this study marks a significant step in the era of personalized medicine. By identifying who truly benefits from chemotherapy, doctors can ensure that patients receive the right care at the right time, improving both their survival rates and their quality of life.
paragraphs.zh: 值得注意的是，這項檢測並不適用於所有人。《衛報健康版》釐清，對於部分患者而言，化療仍是其治療計畫中必要且重要的一環。這項檢測是一種工具，旨在協助醫師根據患者癌症的特定生物學特性，做出更好、更個人化的決策。隨著醫學界展望未來，這項研究標誌著個人化醫療時代的重要一步。透過辨識出誰能真正從化療中獲益，醫師能確保患者在正確的時間獲得正確的照護，進而提升他們的存活率與生活品質。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To divide into two or more groups or parts.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作形容詞（分裂的），這裡作動詞用。文中：The researchers split the participants into two groups.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To publicly acknowledge someone or something as the reason for a success or achievement.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（學分、信用），這裡作動詞用。文中：She credits the trial for allowing her to live a full life without the long-term effects of chemotherapy.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To deal with or try to solve a problem or challenge.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（地址），這裡作動詞用。文中：He noted that the findings allow doctors to use tumour biology to guide their decisions, rather than relying only on traditional clinical methods. By using this evidence-based approach, health systems can use their resources more effectively, and patients can avoid unnecessary, toxic treatments. He noted that this study addresses a long-standing challenge in cancer care.

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: Groundbreaking genomic test could spare millions of breast cancer patients chemotherapy

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: ‘Like Christmas’: woman’s relief after test finds she can skip chemotherapy

quiz.question: What criteria were used to select the 4,429 participants for the Optima trial?

options.A: They had to be under 40 years old and from the UK.
options.B: They had to be 40 or older and have a hormone-responsive type of breast cancer.
options.C: They had to have already completed chemotherapy and hormone therapy.
options.D: They had to show no signs of cancer return for at least ten years.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，參與者條件為 40 歲以上且患有對荷爾蒙有反應的乳癌（佔全球病例 80%）。選項 A 錯誤，因為年齡限制是 40 歲以上而非以下；選項 C 錯誤，因為試驗是為了比較化療與基因檢測後的治療策略，而非完成治療後才加入；選項 D 錯誤，那是檢測結果預測的目標，而非參與者的篩選條件。

quiz.question: Based on the article, what is a likely consequence of the Optima trial's findings for healthcare systems?

options.A: It will lead to the total elimination of chemotherapy for all cancer patients.
options.B: It will increase the cost of breast cancer treatment significantly.
options.C: It will allow for more efficient allocation of medical resources by avoiding unnecessary treatments.
options.D: It will make hormone therapy obsolete in favor of genomic testing.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第六段，Prof Rob Stein 指出該研究能讓醫療系統更有效地使用資源，避免病人接受不必要的毒性治療。選項 A 錯誤，因為第七段強調化療對某些病人仍是必要的；選項 B 錯誤，文章並未提到成本增加，反而暗示透過精準醫療能提升效率；選項 D 錯誤，基因檢測是用來決定是否需要化療，並非取代荷爾蒙治療。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, what does the word 'tool' refer to in the context of the Prosigna test?

options.A: A physical instrument used to perform surgery.
options.B: A method or resource used to achieve a specific goal.
options.C: A piece of software that replaces doctors' judgment.
options.D: A mechanical device for measuring gene activity.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。在文中，'tool' 並非指手術刀等實體工具，而是指一種「輔助手段」或「評估方法」，用來幫助醫生做出更精準的醫療決策。選項 A 錯誤，因為它不是手術設備；選項 C 錯誤，因為它輔助而非取代醫生的判斷；選項 D 錯誤，雖然它涉及基因分析，但 'tool' 在此處是指整個檢測方案作為決策輔助，而非單指測量儀器。

quiz.question: What is the central message of this article regarding breast cancer treatment?

options.A: Chemotherapy is no longer considered a safe treatment for breast cancer.
options.B: The Optima trial proves that all breast cancer patients can safely skip chemotherapy.
options.C: Genomic testing offers a new way to personalize treatment and avoid unnecessary chemotherapy for some patients.
options.D: Survival rates for breast cancer have increased solely due to the invention of the Prosigna test.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。全文核心在於探討透過基因檢測（Prosigna）來識別哪些病人可以免除不必要的化療，從而進入「精準醫療」時代。選項 A 錯誤，化療仍有其必要性；選項 B 錯誤，文章強調是「部分」病人可安全避免；選項 D 錯誤，文章雖提到存活率數據，但並未將整體存活率的提升歸功於單一檢測，且該檢測僅是輔助工具。
